GS3.trivia: India accounts for about 18% of the world's population but has only 4% of the world's freshwater resources.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology demonstrator.
• This crucial test involved a controlled landing experiment, simulating the return of a space vehicle from orbit.
• The RLV program aims to significantly reduce the cost of space launches by making rockets reusable, similar to aircraft.
• This achievement marks a major step towards developing India's own two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) reusable launch system.
• The technology is vital for future space exploration missions, satellite deployment, and potentially space tourism.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: ISRO: Indian Space Research Organisation, India's national space agency.
RLV-TD: Reusable Launch Vehicle - Technology Demonstrator. Experimental vehicle to test various aspects of RLV technology.
Significance: Reduces launch costs, increases launch frequency, promotes sustainable space access.
Other countries with RLV tech: USA (SpaceX, NASA), China.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's space program and its potential impact on global space access.
Analyze the economic benefits of developing indigenous RLV capabilities, including cost reduction, increased launch frequency, and fostering a private space sector.
Examine the technological challenges involved in designing and operating RLVs, such as thermal protection, autonomous landing, and structural integrity.
Evaluate India's position in the global space race, considering its advancements in RLV technology and its future aspirations in space exploration and commercial launches.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendition of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) descending for a controlled landing.
GS3.trivia: The first successful orbital RLV was the Space Shuttle developed by NASA, which began operations in 1981.

GS3.headline: RBI Keeps Repo Rate Unchanged Amidst Persistent Inflation Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) announced its decision to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• This move signals the RBI's cautious stance, prioritizing inflation control despite signs of moderating economic growth.
• The MPC noted persistent inflationary pressures, particularly from food prices and global commodity fluctuations.
• The decision aims to anchor inflation expectations while supporting the economy's resilience.
• Future policy decisions will remain data-dependent, with a focus on achieving the medium-term inflation target of 4%.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary Policy, Fiscal Policy
prelimsPoints: Repo Rate: The rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
MPC: Six-member committee (3 RBI, 3 govt. nominees) that determines policy rates to achieve inflation target.
Inflation Target: RBI's mandated inflation target is 4% with a +/- 2% band (2-6%).
Stance: 'Withdrawal of accommodation' or 'Neutral' indicate policy direction.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to keep the repo rate unchanged, considering the current economic landscape, inflation dynamics, and global headwinds.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools, particularly the repo rate, in managing inflation and supporting economic growth in India.
Examine the challenges faced by the RBI in achieving its medium-term inflation target, including supply-side shocks, global commodity price volatility, and fiscal policy coordination.
Evaluate the potential impact of this monetary policy stance on various sectors of the economy, such as banking, industry, and consumer spending.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic depicting the RBI logo with upward and downward arrows, symbolizing policy decisions.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and is entrusted with the task of fixing the benchmark policy rate (repo rate).

GS3.headline: Government Launches 'Bharat AI Mission' to Catalyze Indigenous AI Development
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government officially launched the 'Bharat AI Mission', a comprehensive initiative to boost indigenous Artificial Intelligence capabilities.
• The mission aims to create a robust AI ecosystem, focusing on research, innovation, skill development, and ethical AI deployment.
• It includes provisions for establishing National AI Centers of Excellence, promoting AI startups, and developing AI-powered solutions for critical sectors like healthcare, agriculture, and governance.
• Funding of ₹5,000 crore has been allocated for the initial phase, with a strong emphasis on public-private partnerships.
• The 'Bharat AI Mission' is seen as a strategic move to position India as a global leader in AI and leverage technology for inclusive growth.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
GS3.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
prelimsPoints: Bharat AI Mission: Government initiative for AI development.
Key pillars: Research & Development, Compute Infrastructure, Skill Development, Ethical AI, AI Applications.
Funding: Initial allocation of ₹5,000 crore.
Nodal Ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY).
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and key components of the 'Bharat AI Mission' and its potential to transform various sectors of the Indian economy and society.
Analyze the opportunities and challenges associated with developing indigenous AI capabilities, including data availability, talent retention, and ethical considerations.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships and international collaborations in accelerating India's AI journey and ensuring its responsible deployment.
Examine how AI can be leveraged to address India's developmental challenges in areas such as healthcare, education, agriculture, and smart governance.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized graphic showing a brain circuit connected to various sectors like health, agriculture, and education, representing AI's widespread application.
GS3.trivia: India is among the top three countries globally in terms of AI skill penetration and talent availability.

GS3.headline: New Report Highlights Growing Threat of Cyberattacks on Critical Infrastructure in India
GS3.explainer:
• A report by the National Cyber Security Coordinator's office revealed a significant increase in cyberattacks targeting India's critical infrastructure.
• Sectors most affected include energy, finance, telecommunications, and transportation networks.
• The report attributes the rise to sophisticated state-sponsored actors and organized cybercrime groups.
• It calls for enhanced cybersecurity measures, greater public-private collaboration, and investment in cyber resilience.
• The findings underscore the urgent need for a robust national cybersecurity strategy to protect essential services and national security.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: Critical Infrastructure: Assets (physical or virtual) essential for the functioning of a society and economy (e.g., power grids, banking systems).
CERT-In: Indian Computer Emergency Response Team, the national nodal agency for responding to cyber security incidents.
Threat actors: State-sponsored groups, organized cybercriminals, hacktivists.
mainsPoints: Analyze the increasing threat of cyberattacks on India's critical infrastructure and its implications for national security, economic stability, and public safety.
Discuss the current cybersecurity framework in India, including the role of CERT-In and other agencies, and identify its strengths and weaknesses in protecting critical assets.
Examine the challenges in combating sophisticated cyber threats, such as attribution, cross-border nature of attacks, and the rapid evolution of technology.
Suggest comprehensive strategies for enhancing India's cyber resilience, including public-private partnerships, international cooperation, skill development, and legal reforms.
GS3.imageDescription: A digital padlock icon superimposed on a map of India, with lines connecting to various critical infrastructure symbols.
GS3.trivia: The National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) is mandated to protect India's Critical Information Infrastructure.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Basics of Cyber Security, Challenges to Internal Security

GS3.headline: India Pledges Increased Contribution to Green Climate Fund (GCF) at COP30 Preparatory Meet
GS3.explainer:
• India announced a significant increase in its financial contribution to the Green Climate Fund (GCF) during a preparatory meeting for COP30.
• The enhanced pledge underscores India's commitment to global climate action and its role in facilitating climate finance for developing nations.
• The GCF is a critical financial mechanism under the UNFCCC, supporting climate change mitigation and adaptation projects in developing countries.
• India urged developed countries to fulfill their pre-2020 commitments and scale up climate finance to meet the $100 billion goal.
• This move strengthens India's moral standing in international climate negotiations and emphasizes the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Green Climate Fund (GCF): Financial mechanism of the UNFCCC, established to help developing countries finance climate action.
UNFCCC: United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change, international environmental treaty.
COP30: 30th Conference of the Parties to the UNFCCC.
CBDR: Principle acknowledging different capacities and historical responsibilities of countries in addressing climate change.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's increased contribution to the Green Climate Fund (GCF) in the context of global climate finance and its implications for developing countries.
Analyze the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities (CBDR) and India's role in advocating for climate justice in international climate negotiations.
Examine the challenges in mobilizing and deploying climate finance effectively, particularly in meeting the $100 billion goal set by developed nations.
Evaluate the broader implications of such pledges for India's foreign policy and its leadership role in climate action among developing nations.
GS3.imageDescription: A hand holding a sapling with a globe in the background, symbolizing global climate action and green finance.
GS3.trivia: The GCF was established at COP16 in Cancun, Mexico, in 2010.

GS3.headline: Union Cabinet Approves New Scheme for Promoting Millets Production and Export
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved a new comprehensive scheme aimed at boosting millets production, processing, and export from India.
• The scheme includes financial incentives for farmers, support for FPOs, and investment in post-harvest infrastructure and value addition.
• It emphasizes promoting millets as 'nutri-cereals' and integrating them into public distribution systems and mid-day meal programs.
• The initiative aligns with the 'International Year of Millets 2023' momentum and India's goal to become a global hub for millets.
• This policy aims to enhance food and nutritional security, support sustainable agriculture, and increase farmers' income.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Food processing and related industries in India— scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
Public Distribution System— objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security.
prelimsPoints: Millets: Group of small-seeded grasses widely grown as cereal crops; includes Jowar, Bajra, Ragi, Foxtail, Kodo, etc.
International Year of Millets 2023: Declared by UN General Assembly at India's proposal.
Benefits: Drought-resistant, high nutritional value, low carbon footprint.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of millets as 'nutri-cereals' for addressing food and nutritional security challenges in India, especially in the context of climate change.
Analyze the key provisions of the new government scheme for promoting millets production and export, and evaluate its potential impact on farmers' income and agricultural diversification.
Examine the challenges in mainstreaming millets into the Indian diet and agricultural practices, including market linkages, consumer awareness, and processing infrastructure.
Suggest policy measures to further boost the demand and supply of millets, considering their environmental benefits and health advantages.
GS3.imageDescription: Various types of millets displayed in bowls, with wheat and rice in the background, highlighting their importance.
GS3.trivia: India is the largest producer of millets in the world, accounting for about 40% of global production.

GS3.headline: Health Ministry Launches Nationwide Campaign Against Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR)
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Health Ministry launched a comprehensive nationwide campaign to combat Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR).
• The campaign focuses on raising public awareness about the judicious use of antibiotics and promoting hygiene practices.
• It involves collaboration with medical professionals, pharmacists, and public health workers to educate communities.
• AMR is a growing global health threat, making infections harder to treat and increasing mortality rates.
• The initiative aligns with India's National Action Plan on AMR and global efforts to contain the 'silent pandemic'.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
GS3.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: AMR: Antimicrobial Resistance, when microorganisms (bacteria, viruses, fungi, parasites) evolve to withstand antimicrobials (like antibiotics).
Causes: Overuse/misuse of antibiotics, poor sanitation, lack of new drugs.
One Health Approach: Recognizes the interconnectedness of human, animal, and environmental health in combating AMR.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing threat of Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR) as a global public health crisis and its specific implications for India's healthcare system and economy.
Analyze the key components of the nationwide campaign launched by the Health Ministry, evaluating its potential effectiveness in raising awareness and changing public behavior.
Examine the challenges in combating AMR, including irrational use of antibiotics, lack of new drug development, and inadequate infection control practices.
Suggest a multi-sectoral 'One Health' approach to address AMR, incorporating measures in human health, animal husbandry, and environmental sanitation.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic depicting a bacteria resisting an antibiotic pill, symbolizing antimicrobial resistance.
GS3.trivia: World Antimicrobial Awareness Week (WAAW) is observed annually from 18-24 November to raise awareness of global AMR.

GS3.headline: India's Manufacturing PMI Shows Strong Growth in November, Boosting Economic Outlook
GS3.explainer:
• India's Manufacturing Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) registered robust growth in November, reaching its highest level in 18 months.
• The expansion was driven by strong new orders, increased production, and sustained export demand.
• This positive indicator suggests a healthy manufacturing sector contributing significantly to overall economic recovery.
• Employment also saw a modest increase, indicating improving labor market conditions.
• The strong PMI data reinforces a positive economic outlook for India in the coming quarters.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Industrial policy changes and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: PMI: Purchasing Managers' Index, an economic indicator derived from monthly surveys of private sector companies.
Above 50: Indicates expansion; Below 50: Indicates contraction; 50: No change.
Manufacturing Sector: Key component of India's GDP and employment.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the Manufacturing PMI as a leading economic indicator and its implications for India's overall economic health and growth trajectory.
Discuss the factors contributing to the strong performance of India's manufacturing sector in November, including domestic demand, export markets, and government policies.
Examine the challenges that the manufacturing sector continues to face, such as global supply chain disruptions, input cost inflation, and competition.
Suggest policy measures to sustain and further accelerate manufacturing growth, contributing to employment generation and 'Make in India' initiatives.
GS3.imageDescription: A bar graph showing an upward trend in a manufacturing index, with factory outlines in the background.
GS3.trivia: The PMI is compiled by S&P Global for more than 40 economies worldwide.

GS3.headline: Parliament Passes Bill to Amend Forest Conservation Act, 1980
GS3.explainer:
• Parliament passed the Forest (Conservation) Amendment Bill, 2025, modifying the Forest (Conservation) Act, 1980.
• The amendments aim to streamline forest land diversions for strategic projects, particularly those related to national security and public utility.
• It seeks to clarify the applicability of the Act to specific types of land, including those recorded as forest but not notified.
• Environmental groups and tribal communities have raised concerns about potential dilution of forest protection and impact on tribal rights.
• The government asserts that the amendments will balance development needs with conservation efforts and simplify regulatory processes.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Forest (Conservation) Act, 1980: Requires central government approval for diversion of forest land for non-forest purposes.
Key amendments: Exemptions for certain linear projects, land within 100 km of international borders, security infrastructure.
Concerns: Impact on 'deemed forests', tribal communities, and environmental safeguards.
Related Acts: Forest Rights Act, 2006 (FRA).
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the Forest (Conservation) Amendment Bill, 2025, discussing its stated objectives and the concerns raised by environmentalists and tribal communities regarding its potential impact on forest ecosystems and tribal rights.
Examine the historical context and evolution of forest laws in India, highlighting the tension between development imperatives and conservation goals.
Discuss the importance of 'deemed forests' and the implications of the amendments for their protection and management.
Suggest measures to ensure that infrastructure development and national security projects proceed without unduly compromising forest conservation and the rights of forest-dwelling communities.
GS3.imageDescription: A dense forest with a blurred image of parliamentary building in the background, symbolizing the intersection of law and environment.
GS3.trivia: The Forest (Conservation) Act, 1980, was enacted to check deforestation and conserve forest resources.

GS3.headline: Government Considers Introduction of New GST Slab for Luxury and Sin Goods
GS3.explainer:
• The GST Council is reportedly considering the introduction of a new, higher GST slab specifically for luxury and sin goods.
• This move aims to simplify the existing multi-tiered GST structure and potentially increase revenue collection.
• The proposal suggests a slab above the current highest rate of 28%, targeting items like high-end automobiles, tobacco products, and aerated drinks.
• The decision, if approved, would require amendments to the GST Act and could face resistance from certain industries.
• The rationalization of GST slabs is a continuous process aimed at improving tax efficiency and compliance.
gsSubTopics: Government Budgeting.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: GST: Goods and Services Tax, an indirect tax applicable throughout India.
GST Council: Constitutional body chaired by the Union Finance Minister, makes recommendations on GST issues.
Current slabs: 0%, 5%, 12%, 18%, 28% (plus cess on certain items).
Sin Goods: Goods considered harmful, e.g., tobacco, alcohol (though alcohol is outside GST).
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind introducing a new, higher GST slab for luxury and sin goods, and its potential impact on revenue generation and consumption patterns.
Analyze the implications of GST slab rationalization for various industries, consumers, and the overall tax structure in India.
Examine the functioning of the GST Council in making key decisions related to the indirect tax regime and the challenges of achieving consensus among states.
Evaluate the effectiveness of GST in achieving its objectives of simplifying taxation, boosting economic growth, and enhancing tax compliance since its implementation.
GS3.imageDescription: A stack of currency notes with a calculator and a GST logo, symbolizing tax collection and economic policy.
GS3.trivia: The Goods and Services Tax (GST) was implemented in India on 1 July 2017.
